event: long-standing history of intractable migraine headaches
admitted to institution's specialty headache unit
no known history of peripheral vascular disease
no known history of cardiac disease
multiple admissions for treatment of headaches
received dihydroergotamine mesylate (DHE) 45 during previous hospitalizations
received two courses of DHE during admission
DHE 1 mg every 8 h for eight doses
second course of DHE on days 7–9
several hours after final dose of second round became obtunded
abdominal x-rays showed mild prominence of the colon
progressively acidotic over next several hours
bedside colonoscopy showed diffuse ischemic colitis
peritoneal cavity filled with murky, foul-smelling fluid
necrosis from splenic flexure to sigmoid colon
large amount of impacted stool in left colon
vasculature intact with audible Doppler signals
final pathology showed 86 cm of patchy dark green/black large bowel
ischemic colitis with thrombi or emboli noted
no signs of venous congestion or stasis
ischemic colitis secondary to high-dose ergotamine use
